Urban Planning · GIS Analysis · Spatial Design

Urban Health Units —
Green space as
infrastructure for living

Dense urban neighborhoods lack accessible, functional green space. In Beijing's Shangdi tech district, we asked: what if green space was designed not as decoration, but as a measurable health system?

📐 Academic Studio Project · Cooperative · Beijing 2021
9.5
km² planning area in Shangdi, Beijing
67K
Resident population served
300m
Target: see green within 300m on foot
3
Planning scales: macro · meso · micro
scroll
Role
Urban System Designer · Spatial Researcher
Contribution
Concept · GIS analysis · Functional unit design · Planning framework
Tools
ArcGIS · Rhino · Illustrator · Photoshop
Timeline
Sep – Dec 2021 · Academic Studio
01

Background & Site Analysis

Shangdi: a tech district
underserved by green

Shangdi is a high-density information technology hub in Beijing — home to Zhongguancun Software Park and Biomedical Park. Despite its density of workers, students, and residents, green space access is poor, unevenly distributed, and not designed around health outcomes.

01 Background — Location: Shangdi area, Haidian District, Beijing · Total area 9.5 km², planning area 18.1 km² · Resident population 67,139 · Maximum E–W distance 3.3 km, N–S 4.6 km · Road network: one-plate and two-belt types, overall lack of road green space · Green Space Accessibility: 300–400m is the threshold range for green space contact; every 500m increase in distance raises blood pressure by 9% and moderate-to-high blood pressure by 14% · 02 Current Situation — Demographic Analysis: active population includes IT workers, students, teachers, tourists and residents · Baidu heat map shows pedestrian flow peaks during weekday commuting hours, centered on Xierqi subway station
02

Current State & Planning Provisions

Mapping the gaps
in accessibility

GIS overlay analysis revealed that most of Shangdi's green space is clustered on the west side, while northeast and southwest areas lack coverage within 300–500m walking distance. The low green coverage rate near the subway station and industrial parks is the starting point for intervention.

Current Green Space — Green space in Shangdi is mainly concentrated on the west side of Shangdi Information Industry Base; open space for residents and office workers is unevenly distributed · Green Space Accessibility Assessment: GIS overlay shows 300m and 500m buffer zones — areas with unseen greenery below 1ha are mainly in the northeast and southwest · Informal Green Space Surveys: distribution of public parking lots, affiliated green areas in Shangdi, and unbuilt land mapped via Baidu Street View · 03 Planning General Provisions — Planning Perspectives: construct Healthy Green Space Unit System from a health perspective, build health impact path through green space system · Planning Goals: with the goal of creating a "healthy unit" with a good living environment and harmonious society, build an ecological and nature-oriented urban green space system at macro, meso, and micro levels to create a healthy home in the Shangdi area
03

Healthy Green Space Unit Planning

From macro structure
to delineated units

Two urban green space axes — the rail transit corridor and the riverside belt — anchor a macro network of shared green space. From this structure, individual health units are delineated using a grid system overlaid on current land use, producing 26 residential units and 10 industrial R&D units.

04 Healthy Green Space Unit Planning — Macro: Healthy Green Space Unit Structure · Two urban green space axes formed along metro rail transit and the river in the Upland area · Five green space types linked as a system: Orbital Greenbelt Axis (rail transit) · Waterfront Green Space Axis (river) · Community Shared Green Space · Urban Piece Green Space · Points and surfaces interconnected · Middle: Healthy Green Space Unit Composition · Delineation method: Create a grid + Current site overlay = Divided units (Industrial R&D unit / Residential unit) · Land divided into four types: residential, administrative, industrial R&D, commercial · 26 residential units and 10 industrial R&D units delineated, majority under 70 hectares in area
04

Unit Composition & Functional Design

Modular green units
designed for daily life

Each health unit type — residential or industrial — has specific mandatory indicators, park size requirements, and dominant functions. The micro scale assigns each unit one of four leading functions: physical sports, recreation and leisure, social interaction, or healing and wellness, based on who uses the space and how.

Health Unit Composition — 24h Vital Living Area – Health Unit: population served ~50,000–80,000 · land area 40–45ha · service radius 15min (800–1000m) · Mandatory: 300m to see the green, 500m to see the garden · Community Park ≥5ha, Width ≥60m, sports facilities, outdoor sports field >5000m² · Tidal Vitality Industrial Park – Health Unit: population served ~30,000–50,000 · walking radius 15min · Community Park ≥1ha, Width ≥50m, outdoor sports field ≥3000m² · Recommended: open affiliated green areas as pleasure gardens, office rooftops as rooftop gardens · Micro – Healthy Green Space Unit Dominant Function: dominant functions determined by nature of land use and service life within health units · Realization of 300m and 600m to the garden through parking lot conversion and planning site control plan · Four leading functions: Physical sports activities · Recreation and leisure activities · Social interaction activities · Healing and wellness activities · Health Unit Planning Map: full Green Space System Planning for Shangdi Area with functional zoning
